mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with p-value < 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and consumer spending growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or regime-specific IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 4.5%, borrowing costs constrain corporate leverage and equity valuations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP combined with stable consumer prices indicates healthy demand growth without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-CPIAUCSL interaction term fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or joint signal IC falls below 0.03 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside falling unemployment during a recovery phase signals synchronized economic strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Synchronized INDPRO growth and UNRATE declines yield negative 60-day returns in > 55% of recovery periods, or regime-conditional predictive power disappears.
